this round.
Should you have any questions, please let us  know.
Thank you!

*Songie Song, 송송이*

Shopee | KR/JP Business Development (KR/JP SA + ICB)

content.question:
Thanks.



Slide 4

   - Can we always benchmark our YOY growth against platform?
- Can remind me what our full year 2025 growth is expected to be?

And
   how does it compare with platform?
- Our YOY topline grew by 17% but our P&L only grew by 2%?

What is the
   reason for this?

Slide 6

   - Where is growth of JP new sellers coming from?

- I remember we had some plan to help more sellers apply for license to
   go into VN, where did we land on that?

LFF seems to be missing targets by
   quite a bit?

Slide 10

   - How are we faring vs targets for each market right now?

Slide 12 -25

   - I am not sure about the context here. I think for any topic that is
   brought to me, I need to understand what we are trying to do,
   recommendation and impact. Right now, I cannot tell what is being presented
   here



Slide 27 – 29

   - Is there a difference if it is LFF?

Slide 33 – 35

   - Again, not very sure of the context.
   - What is the impact?

Who does it impact, what exactly are we doing, and
   what will the eventual impact be?

Others

   - I am concerned about the growth on several markets which are lagging
   behind platform growth
   - I remember we been trying to launch several programs on JPN front, how
   is that doing and what is the impact
   - I saw Ali doing something with KR sellers, is that worth reviewing?
- I saw TT doing something with KR sellers, what exactly are they doing?
And is it something that we are lacking as well?

And I remember we were pushing for LS and Video growth previously, how is
all of that coming along?

content.answer:
In general, the CN items demonstrate a strong positive relationship
   between PRM and order volume—indicating that increase in PRM is closely
   associated with higher order counts. However, KR items exhibit a more
   inelastic response. That is, increase in PRM does not translate into order
   volume with the same magnitude observed for CN items

Even for markets where KR items do show a positive correlation between
   PRM and order volume, the effect size is minimal, typically less than 1% of
   the corresponding impact seen for CN items in the same market

-> This suggests significant differences in buyer responsiveness between
the two items which aligns with our general understanding of KR items.

CN items are generally price sensitive which makes more responsive to
   PRM campaigns(vs. KR items being premium priced/less price elastic so the
   conversion will be lower unless consumers were already looking for the
   product)

CN items are more value for money which also leads to impulsive buys yet
   KR items are premium thus the buying journey is longer.



Slide 6 Haenle Suh <haenle.suh@shopee.com>

   -

   Where is growth of JP new sellers coming from? Xiaolin Huang
   <xiaolin.huang@shopee.com>
   -

   ~30% of JP ADO growth comes from new sellers. New seller growth is
   largely organic, facilitated by ICB mass education and initiatives:
   -

seller community high quality sellers with background of domestic
      and CB ecommerce; and resellers are the key drivers (~90% of ADO
      contribution to new seller ADO)
      -

ICB revamped multiple communication channels (emails, webinars
      etc.) for pushing sellers open multiple market/shop when onboarding
      -

revamp of onboarding form to highlight PH market -  drive new
      seller ADO in PH + 28% MoM (35% of new seller ADO)
      -

   So in H2, the key focus -  push more for new sellers, and new sellers
   shop/SKU migration. And will share in more detail once ready.
   -

   Joanne Lee <joanne.lee@shopee.com> I remember we had some plan to help
   more sellers apply for license to go into VN, where did we land on that?
   LFF seems to be missing targets by quite a bit?




   -

   Yes, the program is up and running — we currently have 5 sellers
   enrolled in VN (ADO impact: 0.8k), along with sellers from other markets.
   Most are expected to inbound by mid to late July.
   -

   As of Jun 19, LFF ADO target is on track at RR% 99% MTD, but our LFF
   penetration to KRMP RR is relatively low at 81%. Key contributing factors
   include:
   -

      VN:
      -

         Delay in new seller/SKU inbounds due to customs policy tightening
         (June 1–15), which has since been resolved
         -

         LFF order decline from one seller due to lower 3PF SKU exposure
         vs. May (a one-off issue)
         -

         VN topline actuals reached 140% of target, which affected the LFF%
         target
         -

      PH:
      -

         One key seller (Mediheal, LFF ADO impact: -0.2k) has not
         replenished stock since Mar due to seller side manpower
shortage, though
         the target was still included in June
         -

         PH topline actuals were at 121% of target, mostly driven by K-pop
         orders—a category not suited for FBS—affecting LFF% target



Slide 10  Joanne Lee <joanne.lee@shopee.com>

   -

   How are we faring vs targets for each market right now?

As of Jun 19,

   -

   LFF ADO at 15.4k (RR 99% to ADO target), MoM +14%
   -

   26% penetration (RR 81% to penetration target)
   -

   LFF ADO EOM projection lands at 90%, having a projected MoM growth% o

the projected UE may not be healthy given the high LFF penetration
target in H2

and whether the very high LFF% targets set earlier in the year were
realistic


Discussion/Decision to be made: After reviewing the UE for the initial
targets, we found that overall UE was quite healthy (UE=0.95 USD), having
the best UE across all 3 scenarios.

At the market level, SLS showed higher UE than LFF in SG/MY, while LFF
   UE was higher over SLS in TH/PH/VN.

However, the initial targets appear too aggressive given the H1 track
   record.

Therefore, we propose 2 revised target scenarios (scenario 2 and 3),
   guided by two principles: 1) Minimizing impact to UE 2) Ensuring
   projections are realistic yet ambitious. Slides 12–25 show the resulting
   PnL and UE across Scenarios 1, 2, and 3.


Recommendation & Impact:  We recommend proceeding with Scenario 2 (slide

as it is a more realistic projection than Scenario 1—excluding
non-managed sellers and SKUs while still being ambitious—and results in a
smaller negative impact in PnL and UE compared to Scenario 3 (PnL down
1.46%, UE down 2% vs. Scenario 1).

Scenario 2 has a lower UE than Scenario 1 mainly because it assumes a
   lower VN 3PF% (44% → 33%) and a higher VN SLS order share—since VN 3PF has
   a higher UE than VN SLS.

Scenario 2 also has better UE than scenario 3 because it assumes higher
   VN 3PF% vs scenario 3.


Slide 27 – 29

   -

   Is there a difference if it is LFF? Jaeeun Park <jaeeun.park@shopee.com>



   -

   Can lower the selling price when using LFF (e.g. from 7.1usd to 6.3usd)
   but still cannot fully close the gap with local selling price (vs. 5.5usd).
   Pls see below table.



   -

   When using LFF (White channel, ocean), sellers can expect -4%p decrease
   in commission, -33%p decrease in operation cost logistics cost in SG, and
   for MY, -6%p decrease in commission, -30%p decrease in logistics cost
   -

      Cost savings from using LFF can help to close the price gap between
      KR sellers and local sellers, KR selling prices become -1 USD, -0.5
      USD cheaper ([A]-[B]) in SG, MY respectively when using LFF
      -

      Using the consol (ocean) channel results in $0.4 less cost saving
      (avg. of SG/MY) compared to the white channel
      -

      -

         SG don’t have gray channel
         -

   However, need to check sellers’ feedback on using LFF because:
   -

Due to F&B’s relatively short shelf life (ocean shipping can take
      up to 4 weeks), sellers might think it’s risky to inbound, or the WH may
      reject the inbound.
      -

Not many F&B SKUs can meet the ADIS requirements set by the WH,
      and if they are not top SKUs, they may remain as black stock.
      -

White channel is the most cost-effective option but F&B resellers
      are hard to get proper license to use white channel


Notes:

*Price comparison from the SKUs of KR F&B brand’s same skus of KR & Local,
item & model name matching 80%, single-item order (using SLS)

*LFF costs are calculated based on level 2 category = ‘snacks’, under the
assumption that ‘white channel & ocean shipping’ is used



Slide 33 – 35 Songie Song <songie.song@shopee.com>

   -

   Again, not very sure of the context.:


[Background] As shared by TH team during THCB monthly meeting last time,
TISI &FDA requirements will be rolled out to all SKUs throughout the year,
Thus, We looked at KR assortment to get a sense of how many SKUS/ ADO to be
potentially impacted, by when, and potentially how much could be saved, and
what’s our plan on saving more.


   -

   What is the impact? Who does it impact, what exactly are we doing, and
   what will the eventual impact be?


[Impact] It will impact new listing SKUs from July onwards, and existing
SKUs in case that VN gov’t reports. FDA related categories (mostly beauty)
to be impacted, which accounts for 70% out of total KR-TH ADO.

   -

   70% of KRTH ADO’s breakdown as follows:
   -

      30%, already complied. Thus no issue at all
      -

      16%: Structurally possible to comply as they’re operated by brand
      owners; however, this depends on cost efficiency on SKU level  to get the
      FDA license
      -

      24%: highly likely not be able to get the license as operated by
      resellers; resellers can use registered FDA license numbers but having
      legal risks if they don’t get agreement from brand owners, thus
we will not
      proactively push resellers to use it.


[what we’re doing now] we think that quick win is to push ‘not yet
complied’ SKUs operated by brand owners. Thus, we’ll target high ADO
efficient SKUs to push brands to get the license. Key blocker i

LS

  As of YTD, KRCB LS GMV contribution stands at 11.5%, which is higher than
the platform average of 7.0%.

  We’ve also exceeded our target run rate so far and will continue to push
through the second half.

VDO

As of YTD, KRCB VDO GMV contribution stands at 4.0%, which is slightly
below the platform avg of 5.3%.

However, we are quite confident that we can ramp up in 2H. This week, we
aligned with the VN local team on LS/VDO push initiatives and got key
programs confirmed specifically for KR sellers for 2H. Since VN is the most
important market for KR, we plan to use VN as a reference and expand these
programs to the remaining three markets — TH, PH, and MY. (those 4 markets
account for 85% of LS/VDO GMV).

There are mainly 2 reasons that we were a bit slow with Video in 1H.

[1] Low KOL adoption of top order volume items compared to local

: for top 20% SKUs by order volume, KR VDO avg order contribution - 1.1% vs
significantly lower than the local avg of 6.7%.


[2] Low supply of seller generated videos compared to local

: for the same top 20% SKUs, there are approx.49 videos per each local top
SKU, compared to only 10 videos per KR top SKUs - a gap of nearly 5x.


  To address these challenges, we are working on two main tracks for 2H:

[1] Boosting KOL Adoption based on local push program participation

VN: We have aligned with local to ensure top performing KR sellers
participation in the

monthly KOL video push programs starting from July, which is expected to
positively

impact video traffic and order performance.

      (* Program in detail : 1) boosting good qualified KOL videos of KR
sellers with platform

support voucher & exclusive slots. 2) encouraging good qualified KOLs to
create

videos with KR SKUs)

TH, PH, MY:  Based on the local video push program in VN, we plan to roll
out similar

programs in those markets.

[2] Increasing Seller Self-Video Supply

All four key markets (VN, TH, MY, PH) are clearly facing a shortage of KR
seller self-video

 supply. We will continue pitching KR sellers to upload more videos,
supported by the

recent PC video upload rollout and mass communication of conten

content.question:
If we look at actuals, other than TW, all markets are growing slower than
platform

   - MY from Jan to May
   - PH from May to June
   - SG from Jan to June
   - TH from Apr to June
   - VN from Jan to June
   - BR from Jan to June



Do we know the reason why?
And how are we dealing with this?

In general, I think we had 3 main initiatives at the start of the year

   - New seller acquisition (not quite sure how it is doing and impact)
   - Growth via Content (seems ok I think?)
   - Growth via FBS (just missing targets all around)



And in order to grow faster than platform, I am not sure if the above is
enough or if we need to do more. But I am not sure we will hit our year end
projections, if we our actuals has been slower than platform for H1’25.







*From:* Yuna Kwon <yuna.kwon@shopee.com>
*Sent:* Sunday, 29 June 2025 9:16 pm
*To:* Ian Ho <hoi@sea.com>
*Cc:* Songie Song <songie.song@shopee.com>; Kassy Ong <kassy.ong@shopee.com>;
Adelline Ta Suet Yee <adelline.tasy@shopee.com>; Angelica Ocampo <
angelica.ocampo@shopee.com>; Ashley Liang <ashley.liangsq@shopee.com>; Chi
Tran Linh <chi.tranlinh@shopee.com>; Chowon Yun <chowon.yun@shopee.com>;
Emily Xia Yingying <emily.xiayy@shopee.com>; Faye Yan <faye.yan@shopee.com>;
